entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: A legume crop (Arachis hypogea L.) often grown on sandy soils.

entities.name: SANDY SOILS
entities.description: Type of soil characterized by high sand content where peanuts are often grown.

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: Environmental stress caused by lack of water, which can limit crop yield.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production harvested per unit area.

entities.name: NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia, which is sensitive to soil drying.

entities.name: SOIL DRYING
entities.description: The reduction of water content in the soil, leading to plant stress.

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A chemical element essential for plant growth, specifically in vegetative tissues and seeds.

entities.name: VEGETATIVE TISSUES
entities.description: The parts of the plant that are not involved in reproduction, such as leaves and stems.

entities.name: SEEDS
entities.description: The reproductive units of the peanut plant.

entities.name: ICGV86015
entities.description: A specific peanut genotype found to be especially tolerant of soil drying regarding nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: SOIL WATER CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of water present in the soil, which affects the onset of nitrogen fixation decrease.

entities.name: AMINO ACID CONCENTRATION
entities.description: The level of amino acids in the leaves of severely stressed plants.
